dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7978 IH: Cooperative Watershed Management Program Reauthorization Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Ciscomani (for himself and Mr. Neguse) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources

form.official-title: To reauthorize the Cooperative Watershed Management Program, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Cooperative Watershed Management Program Reauthorization Act of 2026.

subsection.text: Section 6001 of the Omnibus Public Land Management Act of 2009 (16 U.S.C. 1015) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating paragraphs (4) through (7) as paragraphs (5) through (8), respectively;
by inserting after paragraph (3) the following:
The term Indian tribe has the meaning given the term in section 4 of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act (25 U.S.C. 5304).
in paragraph (7) (as so redesignated), in subparagraph (B)(v)—

subparagraph.text: in subclause (I), by striking or at the end;
in subclause (II), by inserting or after the semicolon; and
by adding at the end the following:

subclause.text: has ancestral lands within the watershed;

subsection.text: Section 6002 of the Omnibus Public Land Management Act of 2009 (16 U.S.C. 1015a) is amended—

clause.text: in clause (i), by striking interests; or and inserting interests, including Indian tribes;;
by redesignating clause (ii) as clause (iii); and
by inserting after clause (i) the following:
demonstrate significant need due to drought, wildfire, or other natural disaster; or

in subparagraph (A)(i), by striking $100,000 and all that follows through the period at the end and inserting $150,000 each year for a period of not less than 3 years, subject to the sufficiency of applications submitted and the availability of appropriations.;
by redesignating clauses (iv) and (v) as clauses (iii) and (iv), respectively; and
by adding at the end the following:

subclause.text: The Secretary may, at the discretion of the Secretary, issue a continuation of the first-phase grant for not more than 2 additional years if the recipient of the first-phase grant has demonstrated satisfactory performance with implementation of the proposal under the initial grant, as determined by the Secretary.
A grant continued pursuant to subclause (I) shall be in an amount not greater than $150,000 each year, as determined to be appropriate by the Secretary.

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (3)(B)(ii), by striking quantity. and inserting the following: “quantity, including—

subclause.text: technical assistance, such as feasibility, design, preliminary environmental review, and engineering.

subparagraph.text: by adding at the end the following:
make funding opportunities for the program available on a regular basis; and
allow applications for grants under the program to be submitted and evaluated multiple times during a calendar year.

paragraph.text: in subsection (f), in the matter preceding paragraph (1), by inserting , and make available to the public, after House of Representatives; and
by striking subsection (g) and inserting the following:

subsection.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $40,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2027 through 2031.
